Air Force officer arrested at Capitol after calling for Trump’s impeachment
items.summary: Maj. Jason Watson was in uniform when U.S. Capitol Police arrested him. He criticized Trump’s actions in Iran and Venezuela, as well as his immigration policies.

items.title: ¡Así festejó Roberto Martínez el penal de Cristiano Ronaldo para el empate de Portugal!
items.summary: Roberto Martínez celebró con intensidad el penal convertido por Cristiano Ronaldo, gol que significó el empate de Portugal en un partido clave de los cuartos de final de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026.

items.title: Ivan Perišić abre el marcador y da delantera a Croacia
items.summary: Después de varios minutos del segundo tiempo arrancado, tira Stanišić un centro al área desde el márgen que llega a Perišić, quien remata con la izquierda desde el interior del área al centro de la portería.

items.title: Scaloni habla sobre el supuesto favorecimiento a Argentina en el Mundial
items.summary: Lionel Scaloni se refirió a los constantes rumores de que la selección argentina es favorita de la FIFA y beneficiada en arbitrajes, y con llaves de clasificación más fáciles que las de las demás selecciones.

items.title: Néstor Lorenzo agradece el elogio de De la Fuente, pero evita ver a Colombia como favorita
items.summary: El entrenador de la selección cafetera afirmó que se siente elogiado por las palabras de Luis de La Fuente, entrenador de España, pero rechaza asumir el papel de favorita en el Mundial.

items.title: Daredevil climbers in court after Empire State Building stunt
items.summary: The climbers appeared in court to face felony charges. NBC News’ Valerie Castro reports.

items.title: Oyarzabal y De la Fuente celebran el pase de España rozando la perfección ante Austria
items.summary: Oyarzabal firmó un doblete y Luis de la Fuente elogió que España rozó la perfección para avanzar en el Mundial. Por su parte, Sabitzer y Rangnick aceptaron la superioridad de la Roja, destacando que la diferencia de calidad se notó en los detalles.

items.title: ¿Listo para el gran choque? Croacia se prepara para un duelo intenso ante Portugal
items.summary: Los croatas calientan para un enfrentamiento espectacular en Toronto. Se juegan la vida en la Copa Mundial, ¿Será la victoria para Modric o para Cristiano Ronaldo?

items.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding festivities begin
items.summary: Security has ramped up outside of Madison Square Garden as the massive celebrations start. The couple donated 26 million dollars to charities across the U.S. before their big day. NBC News’ Chloe Melas has more.

items.title: Hoy se tendrá que despedir un histórico | Enlace Mundial
items.summary: El tiempo no perdona a nadie y ya sea Cristiano Ronaldo o Luka Modric, uno de ellos jugará su último partido mundialista, luego de una impresionante trayectoria.

items.title: Modric y toda la fuerza croata llegan a Toronto con el objetivo de eliminar a CR7 y Portugal
items.summary: La Selección de Croacia ha llegado lejos en los últimos mundiales y quiere acostumbrar a los aficionados a verlos en las mayores instancias. Luka Modric y compañía tienen en sus manos la eliminación de Portugal.

items.title: José Sá: "Sabemos que va a hacer un juego con muchas emociones para nosotros"
items.summary: El portero de la selección portuguesa resaltó la tranquilidad con la quieren enfrentar un juego que tilda de emocional, y reveló que están muy sorprendidos por el recibimiento de una gran cantidad de portugueses, que les dan aliento.

items.title: De Paul: “Tomaremos el partido con mucha responsabilidad” | Enlace Mundial
items.summary: El jugador argentino asegura que sería un grave error subestimar a la Selección de Cabo Verde, por lo que saldrán si ninguna confianza.

items.title: Apoyados por la afición, Roberto Martínez confía en desequilibrar a Croacia
items.summary: Portugal quiere con las variantes del once titular, encontrar los espacios para vencer al equipo croata, en un juego en el que tendrán el apoyo de la comunidad portuguesa, y con la misión especial de homenajear a Diogo Jota, en el aniversario de su muerte

items.title: House Democrats accuse Trump of 'hijacking' America's 250th birthday for his own gain
items.summary: A 55-page report from House Democrats accuses Freedom 250 of America's birthday celebrations for profit, using questionable fundraising methods. Watchdog groups had already sounded alarms.

items.title: The split driving 2026’s most closely watched Senate race: From the Politics Desk
items.summary: Welcome to From the Politics Desk, a daily newsletter that brings you the NBC News Politics team’s latest reporting and analysis from the White House, Capitol Hill and the campaign trail.

items.title: Maine couple spots a bear chasing a moose calf and helps it escape
items.summary: A Maine couple had an unexpected wildlife encounter while heading out for a day of fishing

items.title: Top Eric Adams adviser Frank Carone scheduled to go to trial in August
items.summary: Prosecutors allege Carone accepted bribes from a hotel owner in exchange for steering a multimillion-dollar contract to operate the hotel as a migrant shelter.

items.title: Christian missionaries have found a new (virtual) mission territory
items.summary: The room may be virtual, but the prayer — and the evangelism — are not.

items.title: Even fans who don't speak Spanish are watching the World Cup on Telemundo. Here's why
items.summary: They may not understand every word, but fans appreciate exuberant announcers who match their enthusiasm. Plus, cameras stay on the field during hydration breaks rather than cutting to commercials.

items.title: Babies born this year to receive ‘Freedom 250’ Social Security cards, Trump administration says
items.summary: The limited-edition Social Security cards will be issued only to infants born from July 2 to Dec. 31.

items.title: Utz potato chips issued highest-level FDA recall over salmonella concerns
items.summary: The FDA has classified the recall of some of Utz’s Dirty and Zapp’s brand chips as Class 1, its most serious designation.

items.title: U.S. Olympic canoeist David Hearn charged with damaging Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
items.summary: Several people have been arrested and cited after Trump, without evidence, blamed vandals for destroying the pool.

items.title: Puyallup Tribe and First Nations in Canada hope World Cup partnerships raise cultural awareness
items.summary: A handful of Indigenous tribes are partnering with World Cup host cities to showcase their traditions and cultures

items.title: Massachusetts city with large Cape Verdean community sets curfew ahead of World Cup match
items.summary: A Massachusetts city home to one of the nation’s largest Cape Verdean communities will impose a nighttime curfew ahead of Cape Verde’s World Cup match against Argentina

items.title: What to know about the Society of St. Pius X, the schismatic group excommunicated by the Pope
items.summary: The Society of St. Pius X was excommunicated by Pope Leo for ordaining bishops without his permission. Its issues with the church run far deeper.

items.title: The Dan Sullivan saga in the Alaska Senate race is under investigation, sources say
items.summary: Dan J. Sullivan, a former teacher, is mounting an effort to unseat Sen. Dan S. Sullivan, who has been in office since 2015.

items.title: Photos: Fans gather in the Bay Area for the World Cup match between the U.S. and Bosnia-Herzegovina
items.summary: World Cup watch parties in Santa Clara and the San Francisco Bay Area brought fans together for the United States vs. Bosnia-Herzegovina match, where the U.S. earned a 2-0 win to reach the Round of 16.

items.title: Tiger Woods’ hospital records will be handed over to prosecutors in Florida DUI case, judge rules
items.summary: A judge has ruled that prosecutors are allowed to review medical records related to Tiger Woods’ March vehicle crash and subsequent arrest in Florida on suspicion of driving under the influence

items.title: The days are hot, but so are the nights. That's a problem, experts say
items.summary: As much of the Eastern U.S. experiences potentially record-breaking daytime temperatures, the nights are also staying unusually warm, leaving the human body no time to recover.

items.title: The Supreme Court tackled race, history and the law in fraught and reflective major rulings
items.summary: The Supreme Court has wrapped up a term with significant rulings on race and discrimination

items.title: Wall Street rises toward the finish of its best week in nearly 2 months after bond yields relax
items.summary: U.S. stocks are holding relatively steady after the latest update on the job market suggested the Federal Reserve may feel less pressure to hike interest rates

items.title: US employers pull back on hiring in June amid elevated inflation, global turmoil
items.summary: U.S. employers pulled back on hiring last month and added only 57,000 jobs, less than half the previous month’s total and a sign companies still have a cautious economic outlook

items.title: Shark attack on Alabama teen inspires the start of a national alert system
items.summary: Lulu Gribbin survived a near-fatal shark attack in 2024

items.title: Trump denies conflict of interest over crypto. And, Vatican excommunicates rebel group
items.summary: Trump and his family earned over $1 billion last year through cryptocurrency ventures and other businesses. And, the Vatican declared that the traditionalist Society of St. Pius X has entered schism.

items.title: Former ethics lawyer says Trump's crypto poses 'clear conflict of interest'
items.summary: Former White House ethics lawyer Richard Painter says President Trump "stands alone" in having substantial financial conflicts of interest and that, "for every other executive branch official, it would be a violation."

items.title: Ransom notes in Nancy Guthrie case still being investigated as legitimate, FBI says
items.summary: The FBI has discounted some of the ransom notes that surfaced after the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie as extortion attempts

items.title: Going outside in this heat? Follow these guidelines to stay safe
items.summary: Man, it's a hot one! Don't go out in this summer's heat wave before you arm yourself with these tips and a really big water bottle.

items.title: The U.S. healthcare system is in crisis. A Supreme Court ruling could make things worse
items.summary: Mass deportations would be felt across hospitals and emergency rooms, which already face persistent staffing shortfalls. The long-term healthcare sector will suffer the greatest disruptions, experts say.

items.title: Russia launches deadly hourslong attack on Kyiv
items.summary: Russia launched an hourslong missile and drone attack on Kyiv that killed more than a dozen people across the Ukrainian capital. Military officials said damage was reported in 30 locations across the city, adding that mainly residential buildings and civilian infrastructure were struck.

items.title: Tropical storm Douglas forms in Pacific Ocean, no threat to land: Hurricane center
items.summary: Tropical Storm Douglas has formed in the Pacific Ocean

items.title: 16 children rescued from Ohio home were 'almost feral,' authorities say
items.summary: Authorities say 16 children from the same family who were rescued from a run-down home in rural Ohio were living in horrific conditions and were confined to just one room over much of the past four years

items.title: Head of the Chicago Police Department to retire after 3 years in the position
items.summary: The head of the Chicago Police Department has announced that he will retire after a short tenure leading the nation’s second-largest police force

items.title: John Dickinson’s missing 1776 signature haunts his legacy
items.summary: Jane Calvert has spent 25 years championing John Dickinson, a lesser-known Revolutionary War figure

items.title: Estados Unidos con diez hombres clasifica a octavos tras vencer a Bosnia y Herzegovina
items.summary: Estados Unidos sufrió lo indecible frente al representativo bosnio, pero con goles de Folarin Balogun y Malik Tillman, logró su pase a la segunda ronda de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026, a pesar de jugar con diez hombres gran parte del segundo tiempo.

items.title: Extraordinary heat in US Northeast arrives to clash with Fourth of July revelry
items.summary: Multiday warnings of extreme heat have landed in New York, Boston and Philadelphia

items.title: Tielemans no falla desde los once pasos para Bélgica
items.summary: Tras la revisión del VAR, el árbitro señaló penal por una falta sobre Youri Tielemans dentro del área. El mediocampista belga tomó el balón y convirtió desde los once pasos para darle la ventaja a su selección en la prórroga.

items.title: NYC police are bracing for July 4, World Cup action, searing heat -- and maybe a pop star's wedding
items.summary: The New York Police Department is bracing for a slew of massive Independence Day celebrations along with a World Cup match this weekend, scrambling thousands of officers on land, sea and air to handle security measures as an intense heat wave bears dow...

items.title: Democratic congressman asks the CFPB to investigate 'rent now, pay later' companies
items.summary: A Democratic member of Congress is calling for a federal investigation into the "rent now, pay later" industry

items.title: Federal Reserve Chair Warsh emphasizes political independence, signals focus on inflation
items.summary: New Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said Wednesday that the central bank would remain independent and seek to bring down inflation, likely foreclosing the rate cuts President Trump has sought

items.title: Marine heat wave caused seabird deaths off California. El Nino could worsen die-off
items.summary: Many seabirds are starving to death as a marine heat wave lingers off California and fish seek deeper, cooler waters

items.title: Judge orders Pentagon to lift policy that NYT journalists be accompanied by an escort
items.summary: A federal judge has ordered the Pentagon to lift its requirement that New York Times journalists be accompanied by an escort while in the building

items.title: Book that Tupac Shakur murder suspect wrote is clear for use in trial, judge rules
items.summary: A judge has ruled that a book co-written by Duane Davis, who prosecutors allege ordered the 1996 killing of rap icon Tupac Shakur, will be used in trial

items.title: The best 4th of July sales to shop before the holiday weekend
items.summary: The best 4th of July sales are happening now. Our editors found worthwhile deals on Apple, Yeti, Medicube and more.

items.title: Mississippi district attorney pleads guilty to bribery conspiracy and resigns
items.summary: A Mississippi district attorney has pleaded guilty to bribery conspiracy and resigned his post

items.title: How to talk about money with your kids
items.summary: Talking to kids about money can be fraught, especially if parents don’t feel totally comfortable with the topic themselves

items.title: A Jefferson for every era, from Lincoln to Trump, and the contradictions that endure
items.summary: Jon Meacham is among many historians reflecting on the complex legacy of Thomas Jefferson

items.title: Jury finds alleged MS-13 members guilty of murder and racketeering in Nevada
items.summary: Three men who prosecutors say were members of the El Salvador-based gang MS-13 have been convicted on dozens of charges

items.title: Trump's fixation on voting has had mixed results. He still has ways to affect November's elections
items.summary: President Donald Trump has tried many ways to tighten his grip on U.S. elections, from signing executive orders to pushing restrictive legislation in Congress

items.title: Game on: NBA prepares for 2026 draft
items.summary: Fresh off the NBA Finals, teams are preparing for the 2026 draft where fresh talent will get to shine. NBC News' Marquis Francis reports on what to expect and how the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade is shaking up the league.

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
items.summary: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit
items.summary: A Florida man who served three decades behind bars for a murder he says he didn't commit returned to prison Monday after spending the past two years building a life outside prison walls.

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

items.title: Tennessee Air National Guardsman applied to be a hitman online, the FBI says. It was a spoof website and now he's facing charges
items.summary: A Tennessee Air National Guardsman is facing charges after applying to be a hitman on a spoof "rent-a-hitman" website, according to the Department of Justice.

items.title: Even when wives make as much as husbands, they still do more at home
items.summary: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security
